title: Emit `cite_policy_activated` marker idempotently on first --cite-coverage invocation
description: Add a one-time idempotent marker emit at the entry of the new runDoctorCiteCoverage flow: check readEventLedger({event_type:'cite_policy_activated'}) — if empty, appendEventLedgerEvent with current policy_version (read from packages/cli/package.json or BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL hash). Best-effort: failure to emit must NOT abort the report. This marker becomes the ts-floor for all downstream coverage queries.

files.target: new helper `ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker(projectRoot)` near other event-emit helpers + invocation site inside future runDoctorCiteCoverage (also called from runDoctorReport for symmetry if cite-coverage runs as part of full report — but locked decision says flag → fast-path, so primary call site is runDoctorCiteCoverage entry)
files.change: Define `ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker(projectRoot: string): Promise<{ marker_ts: number, emitted_now: boolean }>`. Logic: (1) call readEventLedger(projectRoot, {event_type:'cite_policy_activated'}) wrapped in try/catch — on read error return {marker_ts:0, emitted_now:false}. (2) If events.length > 0: return {marker_ts: events[0].ts, emitted_now:false}. (3) Else: build payload {kind:'fabric-event', id:randomId, ts:Date.now(), schema_version:1, event_type:'cite_policy_activated', policy_version: getPolicyVersion(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString()}. Call appendEventLedgerEvent(projectRoot, payload) wrapped in try/catch — on write error return {marker_ts:0, emitted_now:false}; on success return {marker_ts: payload.ts, emitted_now:true}. Export helper for testing.

Add `import { readEventLedger, appendEventLedgerEvent } from './event-ledger'` if not already imported; import policy version source (e.g. import bootstrap constant or read package version at runtime).

Add 3 tests: (a) first call emits marker, returns emitted_now:true with marker_ts ≈ Date.now(); (b) second call (after marker exists) returns emitted_now:false with marker_ts from existing event; (c) read failure → returns {marker_ts:0, emitted_now:false} silently (never throws). Use existing createInitializedProject + writeFile patterns.

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts existing emit helpers (e.g. emitDoctorRunEventBestEffort or similar) to confirm best-effort pattern.

Define getPolicyVersion(): string helper — return current Fabric version (read packages/cli/package.json.version OR hardcode '2.0.0-rc.20'; choose simpler — hardcoded literal updated by TASK-11 release).
Implement ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker: readEventLedger filter → if 1+ events, return first.ts; else appendEventLedgerEvent + return new ts.
Wrap both reads and writes in try/catch (best-effort: never throw, return zero marker_ts on error so caller can skip coverage gracefully).
Export the helper for unit test access.
Write 3 unit tests covering: empty-ledger first-emit, populated-ledger no-emit, error-path silent-zero.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor` to confirm green.

reference.examples: emitDoctorRunEventBestEffort is the canonical pattern — never propagates failures.

rationale.chosen_approach: Emit marker at first --cite-coverage invocation (not at install). Lazy creation = clean test seeding (empty events.jsonl is valid initial state), users who never run --cite-coverage incur zero events.jsonl pollution.
alternatives_considered: Emit at install time (rejected: install hot path pollution, harder to test in isolation, marker would exist for users who never use cite policy)
Emit on every doctor run (rejected: would create duplicate markers; the locked design says 'idempotent: check first')
decision_factors: Idempotent: check-before-emit pattern matches MEMORY locked rule
rationale.tradeoffs: First --cite-coverage run will show 0% coverage (marker_ts = now, no historical events to count). Expected behavior — communicated via report header 'cite policy activated at <ts>'.

manual_checks: Run helper twice in succession in a clean test workspace; assert first call emits, second doesn't
Seed events.jsonl with a pre-existing marker manually; assert helper returns existing marker_ts
Corrupt events.jsonl with malformed line; assert helper returns {marker_ts:0, emitted_now:false} without throwing
success_metrics: ensureCitePolicyActivatedMarker is idempotent across N calls (only 1 marker line written)
Returns marker_ts > 0 when marker exists or was just emitted
Returns marker_ts === 0 only on read/write failure

criteria: First invocation on empty ledger writes exactly 1 cite_policy_activated event and returns emitted_now:true with marker_ts ≈ Date.now() ± 100ms
Second invocation on populated ledger writes NO new event and returns emitted_now:false with marker_ts equal to original event ts
Read failure (corrupted ledger) returns {marker_ts:0, emitted_now:false} without throwing
Write failure (read-only fs) returns {marker_ts:0, emitted_now:false} without throwing
Emitted event passes Zod validation per citePolicyActivatedEventSchema (TASK-02)
All 3 new tests pass in doctor.test.ts
Commit msg: `feat(rc20): emit cite_policy_activated marker on first run (TASK-04)`
